metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC4's f088-gated phrasing with an in-session equivalent (e.g., 'Running the completeness shell script from AC1 exits 0 with no missing-file output') and move the f088 dependency note to a non-AC remark so it does not masquerade as a closeable criterion.
findings.finding: AC4 ('the docs-index CI gate... passes once wired') is not evaluable in-session for this ticket because f088 — which provides the CI gate — is explicitly not required to exist when this ticket is completed. This makes AC4 a deferred, external-dependency-gated criterion rather than an in-session verifiable one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'The index is complete and correct such that the docs-index CI gate added by sibling f088... passes once wired'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit audit step (e.g., `grep -r 'bash-migration\|dco-rollout-evidence\|88ab-feature-branch' docs/` before git-mv) and enumerate all inbound-link files in the plan so each is updated or verified as part of this ticket's scope.
findings.finding: The plan says 'update inbound links' for the git-mv'd files but never enumerates or audits which files currently link to dco-rollout-evidence.md, 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md, and bash-migration.md. Without an explicit audit step, inbound references in other docs (e.g., CLAUDE.md, other .md files, CI configs) will silently break after the move, stranding stale references.
findings.location: Scope section — 'move those files under docs/archive/ (git-mv, update inbound links)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Before relocating, run `grep -rn "bash-migration\|dco-rollout-evidence\|88ab-feature-branch" docs/ $(git ls-files)` and list every file with an inbound link; update each as part of this ticket.' Add those files to the Files section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and fix all inbound references to `docs/bash-migration.md` before git-mv: at minimum `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py`, `_engine_support/validate.py`, `_engine_support/next_batch.py`, `_store/lock.py`, `_commands/composer.py`, `_commands/leaf.py`, `_commands/__init__.py`, `_commands/unlink.py`, `_commands/_seam.py`, root `README.md`, `docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md`, `docs/archive/README.md`, `docs/archive/tier-e-execution-plan.md`, and four test-file module docstrings.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'update inbound links' after git-mv of bash-migration.md to docs/archive/, but the actual inbound link count is large and unaudited: at least 10 references exist in src/ production docstrings (src/rebar/_lib_writes.py, _engine_support/validate.py, _engine_support/next_batch.py, _store/lock.py, _commands/composer.py, _commands/leaf.py, _commands/__init__.py, _commands/unlink.py, _commands/_seam.py), the root README.md, and docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md. The plan's scope bullet does not enumerate these, making it unverifiable whether the implementer will find and fix them all.
findings.location: Scope bullet: 'Candidates: dco-rollout-evidence.md, 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md, bash-migration.md' / Files section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit audit step: run `grep -r 'bash-migration.md' .` before the git-mv and list all hits as an AC sub-bullet. After the move, either (a) update every reference to the new path `docs/archive/bash-migration.md`, or (b) leave the file in place and only add the 'Historical evidence' section label in the index (no git-mv), since the file is already referenced by section-number anchors in production code.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete in-session proving command for each AC (e.g., for AC1: `for f in docs/*.md; do grep -qF "$(basename $f)" docs/README.md || echo "MISSING: $f"; done`; for AC2: confirm the 'Historical evidence' section header exists in docs/README.md via `grep -q '## Historical' docs/README.md`).
findings.finding: No proving command is specified for any acceptance criterion. The only mentioned verifier — the docs-index CI gate from sibling ticket f088 — is explicitly not available during this ticket's execution ('does NOT require f088 to exist first'). There is no fallback shell command to confirm completeness of the index or correct link structure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate ALL inbound-link consumers outside `docs/` for each git-mv candidate (`bash-migration.md`, `88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md`) — specifically the src/rebar/ source docstrings, test file headers, root README.md, docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md, and infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh — and add them to the plan's Files section with explicit update notes.
findings.finding: The plan says 'update inbound links' for the git-mv'd files but never enumerates the consumers outside docs/ that will need updating. bash-migration.md alone is referenced in 8+ src/rebar/ source-file docstrings, 3+ test files, root README.md, and docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md. 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md is referenced in infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh. None of these cross-directory callers are listed in the plan's Files section.
findings.location: Scope section / Files list: 'plus relocated/updated: docs/dco-rollout-evidence.md, docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md, docs/bash-migration.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Files section to list: src/rebar/_commands/composer.py, src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py, src/rebar/_commands/unlink.py, src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py, src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py, src/rebar/_engine_support/next_batch.py, src/rebar/_store/lock.py, src/rebar/_lib_writes.py, tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py, tests/interfaces/queries/test_next_batch_compute.py, tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py, tests/interfaces/facades/test_leaf_writes_parity.py, README.md, docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md, infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh — all require updating if bash-migration.md or 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md are relocated.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'and/or' structural ambiguity: state the default approach (e.g., 'add a labeled section in the index; git-mv is optional and only if the team prefers archive separation') so the executor has a single decision to act on.
findings.finding: The plan defers to the executor the structural decision of whether historical evidence files should receive only a labeled section in the index OR be physically relocated via git-mv to docs/archive/ (with inbound links updated). These are meaningfully different implementations with different link-maintenance consequences, and the 'and/or' in both the Scope and AC2 does not resolve the choice.
findings.location: Scope bullet 2: 'a "Historical evidence" section in the index and/or move those files under docs/archive/'; AC: 'section label and/or docs/archive/ relocation with links updated'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the scope bullet to: 'Give historical evidence a clearly-labeled home by adding a "Historical evidence" section in docs/README.md. Optionally (but not required for AC) git-mv the files to docs/archive/ and update inbound links — if done, all inbound links must be updated.' Then align AC2 to match the mandatory path only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the reference to `docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md` in `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh:348` when git-mv'ing that file to `docs/archive/`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that 88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md can be git-mv'd with 'update inbound links', but one inbound reference exists in infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh:348 (a comment `# concurrency.group cancel-in-progress (docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md AC5)`) that the plan does not enumerate.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` to the files list in the plan, or add a grep audit step (`grep -r '88ab-feature-branch-evidence' .`) as part of the implementation checklist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out statement for the `git-mv` operations (e.g., 'if external links are found, a redirect stub or git-revert is the back-out path') and clarify whether 'inbound links' scope includes CI scripts and external URLs.
findings.finding: The plan performs `git-mv` of three files to `docs/archive/` (a path-breaking change for any consumer holding direct references) and acknowledges updating inbound repo links, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan if the move causes breakage (e.g., external site links, CI scripts referencing old paths).
findings.location: Scope — second bullet ('Give historical evidence a clearly-labeled home: … git-mv, update inbound links'); Files section listing `dco-rollout-evidence.md`, `88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md`, `bash-migration.md`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Scope or a new 'Rollback' note: 'If the git-mv breaks any consumer outside this repo, revert the move via `git revert` and instead use a labeled section in `docs/README.md` without relocation. Inbound-link scope: only repo-internal `.md` files and CI config; external URLs are out of scope and accepted as broken.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
